Methodology Track Record Case Studies Library About Engage

Contact

One address. Six routes. A written paragraph is enough.

The firm runs a single canonical address, routed by subject line. There is no contact form. A written paragraph naming the principal, the decision under consideration, and the horizon is the form the firm prefers — because the intake review is itself part of the engagement standard.

admin@power-in-numbers.net · Reply within 5 business days

§ 01 The address

Canonical address

admin@power-in-numbers.net

One address handles every inbound. The subject-line conventions below are how the firm routes internally — they are not required, but they accelerate triage. If none of the routes match, write anyway; the firm will route the question.

§ 02 Six routes

Match the subject line to the inquiry.

Engage the firm

Begin a written intake for one of the three engines (the Market Entry Intelligence Decision Protocol, VERIDEX BI USA, the Sovereign Intelligence Decision Protocol).

Subject line

Engage · [Engine] intake

Typical sender

Individuals, firms, sovereign principals.

Compose → Three engagement paths →

Press and media

Press queries with deadlines, boilerplate verification, embargo arrangements.

Subject line

Press · [Outlet] [Deadline]

Typical sender

Working press, journalists, editors.

Compose → Press kit →

Speaking and convenings

Invitations to summits, multilateral panels, keynote engagements.

Subject line

Speaking · [Convening] [Date]

Typical sender

Convening institutions, conference organisers.

Compose → About the firm →

Curriculum and instruction

Inquiries about the eight-phase Power In Numbers University curriculum.

Subject line

Curriculum · [Cohort] inquiry

Typical sender

Operators, faculty, institutional partners.

Compose → Curriculum →

Library and citations

Academic citation, library entry references, primary-source verification.

Subject line

Library · [Entry] citation

Typical sender

Academics, researchers, students.

Compose → Library →

Routing question

When the right path is not obvious — for example, an operator working with a sovereign convener.

Subject line

Engage · Routing question

Typical sender

Senders unsure which path applies.

Compose → Three engagement paths →
§ 03 How the firm handles inbound

Written first.

The firm prefers a written paragraph naming the principal, the decision, and the horizon to a structured form. The intake review is itself part of the engagement standard — a request that arrives well-named is half-answered.

One address.

There is one address: admin@power-in-numbers.net. The subject-line conventions above are how the firm routes internally. Senders who do not match a route should still write — the firm will route the question.

Five business days.

The firm replies within five business days for engagement intake and routing questions. Press queries with declared deadlines are escalated.

No phone intake.

The firm does not run intake by phone. Once a written intake is accepted, scheduled calls follow on a confirmed basis. This protects the audit-trail standard the firm holds itself to.

§ 04 Operating particulars

Firm

Power In Numbers

Principal

Jay Davis · Founder

Site

power-in-numbers.net

General inquiries

admin@power-in-numbers.net

Power In Numbers A strategic consultancy. Practiced as decision cartography.
